title: Add override of SignaturePolicyPath
body:
Using podman with paths overridden by various CONTAINERS_* environment
variables still requires 'policy.json' to be present in '/etc/containers'
for root and in '$HOME/.config/containers' for rootless set-ups.

This commit adds the environment variable 'CONTAINERS_POLICY_JSON' to
override this path like all other similar paths.

comments.body:
Compare https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1264 for context.

Isn’t there a _single_ place where this policy decision could be made, instead of copy&pasted like this, which is certainly going to end up inconsistent?

At the very least it should be a function instead of copy&pasted 8 lines. But isn’t there a pre-existing policy point? Maybe `containers/common/pkg/config.DefaultConfig`? (I haven’t read all the relevant Podman code.)

Unfortunately, DefaultPolicy is definitely not enough. I tested that point first of all, and it appears that the aforementioned CLI options pass an empty string if they are not explicitly set by shell user, and that empty string value propagates down till the image library. I agree, however, about making it a function in common place. Thanks for pointing this out, again! :)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *basilgello* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please ask for approval from **baude** after the PR has been reviewed. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I checked the callgraphs of affected commands and it seems there is no way to route all of them via single.path:

Look at https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/libimage/pull.go#L48, I think the change should go in there.

@rhatdan Thanks for the hint! However, `podman build` still fails:

```
$ podman build -f /tmp/Containerfile
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/library/busybox
STEP 2: RUN date +%s > /ts
STEP 3: COMMIT
Error: error committing container for step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[date +%s > /ts] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN date +%s > /ts Original:RUN date +%s > /ts}: error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory
```
